title: selago thomsonii var. coerulea
intro: Selago thomsonii var. coerulea, also known as Blue Cape Crested Cabbage, is a type of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to the Western Cape Province of South Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been shown to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiseptic, and anti-cancer properties, and it has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Selago thomsonii var. coerulea are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
